title: A Year of Firsts
scripture_text: A time to weep, and a time to laugh; a time to mourn, and a time to dance.
reflection: The firsts are the hardest. The first morning, the first birthday, the first holiday without them. Each one is a small dying and a small living.
body:
Light a candle. Or do not. Both are fine.

The first year after loss is full of landmines. Ordinary moments ambush you. A song on the radio. A smell from a kitchen. The way someone laughs in a checkout line.

You will think you are doing well, and then something will break you open. This is not failure. This is the heart updating its map of the world.

Do not rush the year. Do not pretend you are past it. The Lord, who numbers the very hairs of your head, is numbering these days with you. They are precious in His sight, even when they feel unbearable.

When the laughter returns, let it. When the dance returns, let it. Grief is not a betrayal of love. It is the price of it.
prayer: Lord, this year has been long. Be patient with me. Let me love well, even now. Amen.
